verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific single-pass transmembrane protein (type I) belonging to the Ig-like cell adhesion molecule family. On mature spermatozoa it resides in the acrosomal membrane intracellularly before acrosome reaction; upon acrosomal exocytosis it redistributes to the sperm plasma membrane outer face, where it is essential for binding and fusion with the oocyte. Its surface exposure is therefore cell-state/event-dependent (acrosome-reacted sperm), qualifying as contextual via cell_state_induced rather than constitutive surface display.
